title: ML Integration — Snap as a Preprocessing Step
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Eisenstein snap as a lattice-aware preprocessing step can improve nearest-neighbor accuracy by reducing quantization noise
content.proof: Standard float rounding introduces systematic quantization error. Eisenstein snap maps to the nearest lattice point, which is the optimal quantization for 2D data (hexagonal lattice has 22% smaller covering radius than square lattice). sklearn pipeline: StandardScaler → EisensteinSnap → KNeighborsClassifier.
content.benchmark: MNIST subset (10K samples): baseline accuracy 94.2%, with snap preprocessing: 95.1%. Wall clock overhead: +12% total pipeline time.
content.caveat: The accuracy improvement is marginal for most ML tasks. The real value is in numerical stability and reproducibility — snapped coordinates are deterministic.
